enonce.texte:
Lesson 26: Compound Adjectives
Read the rules and choose the correct option to complete each sentence.
Rule reminder: Compound adjectives are formed by joining two words (often with a hyphen) to describe a noun. Common patterns: adjective + noun (well-known), number + noun (five-minute), noun + adjective (world-famous), noun + past participle (sun-kissed), adjective + past participle (easy-going).

questions.texte: Identify the correct compound adjective to describe 'a break that lasts ten minutes'.

sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect: 'minutes' should be singular in a compound adjective.
Incorrect: Possessive form is not used here.

questions.texte: Choose the correct sentence that uses a compound adjective.

sousQuestions.texte: She is a well known actress.
sousQuestions.reponse: Incorrect: Missing hyphen: 'well-known' before a noun.
Incorrect: Missing hyphen and article: 'a hard-working student'.

sousQuestions.texte: They live in a densely-populated city.
sousQuestions.reponse: Correct: Adverb + past participle compound adjective.

questions.texte: Match the two columns to form the correct compound adjective.

enonce.texte: Situation: You are writing a report about a community development project in your village (Lesson 48). Use the clues in brackets to form the correct compound adjective and complete the paragraph.
images: A group of young volunteers in Cameroon planting trees and cleaning a community well, wearing casual clothes and smiling.

questions.texte: Complete the sentence: 'Our village organized a ________ (purpose: to clean the well) campaign last month.'
Complete the sentence: 'The chief made a ________ (duration: ten minutes) speech about solidarity.'
Complete the sentence: 'Thanks to ________ (adjective: good heart) donors, we bought new textbooks.'
Complete the sentence: 'This project is ________ (verb: need + urgent) because many children miss school due to lack of water.'
Rewrite the following sentence using a compound adjective: 'The young man has a heart of gold. He paid for the clinic.'
questions.reponse: The golden-hearted young man paid for the clinic. (Accept 'kind-hearted' or 'good-hearted')

intitule: Diagnosing Common Errors with Compound Adjectives

enonce.texte: Error Analysis: Four students from Lycée de Bafoussam wrote sentences about education and technology (Lesson 33 & 36). Identify the mistake in each sentence and provide the corrected version.

questions.texte: Student A: 'I bought a twelve-inches laptop for my online courses.'
questions.reponse: Error: 'inches' should be singular in the compound adjective. Correction: 'a twelve-inch laptop'.

questions.texte: Student B: 'Our principal is a well-respected man by all the staff.' (Is this correct? If not, fix it.)
questions.reponse: Correct as is. 'well-respected' is a valid compound adjective.

questions.texte: Student C: 'She gave me an ill-advised suggestion about the scholarship application.'
Student D: 'The government launched a five-years plan to fight malaria.'

intitule: Creative Writing: Describing a Role Model

enonce.texte:
Task: Write 3 complete sentences describing a teacher, parent, or community leader in Cameroon. You must use at least 4 different compound adjectives from the list below. Underline the compound adjectives.

Word bank: hard-working, open-minded, good-looking, well-known, easy-going, record-breaking, cold-blooded, last-minute, part-time, world-famous.
images: A Cameroonian teacher in a classroom pointing at a blackboard, with students raising hands.

questions.texte: Write your three sentences here. (Example format: 'My English teacher is a hard-working and open-minded person...')
questions.reponse: Example answer: 'My father is a hard-working farmer who wakes up at 5 a.m. every day. He is also an open-minded parent who listens to my problems. Last month, he made a last-minute decision to pay my school fees.'
